issues.sample: A REPUTABLE RV DEALERSHIP IN BROWNSTOWN, MI SOLD AND DELIVERED TO ME ON 12 MARCH 2020 A NEW 2020 WINNEBAGO TRAVATO INTEGRATED ONTO A 2019 RAM PROMASTER CHASSIS. AT THE TIME OF DELIVERY THERE WAS AN ACTIVE SAFETY RECALL POSTED BY FCA IN JANUARY OF 2020 ON THE CHASSIS. I WAS NOT IN

WHEN I TAKE MY FOOT OFF THE ACCELERATOR AND PUT IT ON THE BRAKE THE CAR SLOWS DOWN BUT THEN STARTS TO ACCELERATE AGAIN.  ON A CITY STREET AT A STOP SIGN/LIGHT.  A LIGHT WILL BRIEFLY FLASH ON DASH THAT SAYS "HILL HOLDER - SEE SERVICE".  I'VE TAKEN IT TO BE CHECKED.  THEY DROVE IT

The contact owns a 2019 Ram Promaster 3500. The contact received notifications of NHTSA Campaign Numbers: 25V720000 (Engine and Engine Cooling) and 25V552000 (Back Over Prevention); however, the parts to do the recall repairs not yet available. The local dealer was made aware of

WHEN I TAKE MY FOOT OFF THE ACCELERATOR AND PUT IT ON THE BRAKE THE CAR SLOWS DOWN BUT THEN STARTS TO ACCELERATE AGAIN.  ON A CITY STREET AT A STOP SIGN/LIGHT.  A LIGHT WILL BRIEFLY FLASH ON DASH THAT SAYS "HILL HOLDER - SEE SERVICE".  I'VE TAKEN IT TO BE CHECKED.  THEY DROVE IT

On steep road descent, brake warning light came on.  No loss of brake function.  No incident.  Cycling engine off then on clears warning light.  Parking brake was off, and brake fluid was completely full.  No excessive brake heat or smells.  This situation has occurred twice sepa

narrative.headline: What 2019 Ram Promaster owners report most
narrative.summary: Across 56 owner complaints to NHTSA, the most reported area is Power Train (41% of reports). Some reports involve a fire or crash — read the details below.
